title: pea pasta
introduction: A bright, creamy one-pot pasta made with sweet peas, Parmesan or pecorino, and fresh lemon. Blending half the peas creates a silky sauce that coats every bite.
ingredients: 4 cups water (plus up to 1 additional cup as needed)
1 cup grated Parmesan or pecorino cheese
1 lemon, zested (optional) and cut into quarters
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add diced onion and a pinch of salt. Cook for about 5 minutes, until translucent and softened.
Add frozen peas, a generous pinch of salt, black pepper, and 4 cups of water. Bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer and cook for 5 minutes.
Remove about half of the peas with some of the broth. Blend until smooth using a blender or stick blender (add broth as needed to blend smoothly). Return the purée to the pot and stir to combine.

Add the pasta directly to the pot. Cook for about 12 minutes, stirring occasionally, until just shy of al dente. Add additional water as needed (up to 1 cup) to prevent sticking and maintain a smooth, saucy consistency.
Remove from heat. Stir in the grated cheese until melted and creamy.
Add the juice from half the lemon and season with salt. Taste and adjust with more lemon juice and salt as needed (up to the whole lemon).
Serve topped with red pepper flakes and reserved lemon zest, if using.
notes: Zest the lemon before cutting. The pasta will continue cooking slightly from residual heat, so stop just before al dente. Adjust water gradually to maintain a creamy sauce consistency.
